label: qwen36_35b_a3b_gguf_q4ks_w7900_therock_713_comparison

therock_environment.library_resolution_check: ctypes.CDLL("libamdhip64.so") maps to /home/lhl/mambaforge/envs/therock/lib/python3.12/site-packages/_rocm_sdk_devel/lib/libamdhip64.so when env is run with ROOT/bin first and ROOT/lib first.
therock_environment.why_rocm_sdk_test_failed: Current interactive shell has /opt/rocm/bin before the conda env in PATH and /opt/rocm/lib first in LD_LIBRARY_PATH. Therefore rocm_sdk path --root reports the TheRock devel root, but hipconfig --rocmpath resolves /opt/rocm. Use a clean env or conda activation env vars that prepend rocm-sdk path --bin and root/lib.

commands.env_shape: env -i PATH="$ROOT/bin:/home/lhl/mambaforge/envs/therock/bin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/bin:/bin" LD_LIBRARY_PATH="$ROOT/lib:$CONDA_PREFIX/lib/python3.12/site-packages/_rocm_sdk_core/lib:$CONDA_PREFIX/lib/python3.12/site-packages/_rocm_sdk_libraries_gfx110X_all/lib" HIP_PATH="$ROOT" ROCM_PATH="$ROOT" HIP_LIB_PATH="$ROOT/lib" HIP_INCLUDE_PATH="$ROOT/include" HSA_OVERRIDE_GFX_VERSION=11.0.0 PYTHONPATH=. ... bash --noprofile --norc -c ...

therock_7_13_w7900_persistent_4k128_1war.hip_used_peak_gib_note: TheRock hipMemGetInfo reports invalid low/negative process values on this mixed kernel/userspace setup; use tracked_peak_gib for memory comparison.

findings: The previous W7900 benchmarks were not using TheRock 7.13 despite the packages being installed; hipcc and libamdhip64 resolved to /opt/rocm 7.2.3 because PATH and LD_LIBRARY_PATH put /opt/rocm first.
With a clean TheRock 7.13 environment, W7900 4K Q4_K_S prefill improves from 1733.9 tok/s to 2580.5 tok/s (+48.8%) while decode stays essentially unchanged (~101 tok/s).

The TheRock W7900 prefill result is now within ~5.2% of the RX 7900 XTX final-gate prefill result (2580.5 vs 2722.5 tok/s), so the earlier W7900/RX prefill gap was primarily a ROCm 7.2.3 compiler/runtime issue.

Caveat: The first TheRock warmup iteration after session load produced a wrong sampled/final token in repeated runs (e.g. preview [318,...] final 318 or 220), while the second warmup and all measured runs were stable and matched expected final token 85. Treat 7.13 evidence as diagnostic until the first-iteration correctness anomaly is understood or a mandatory warmup is added for serving.
TheRock hipMemGetInfo reports invalid low/negative used memory on this mixed kernel/userspace setup; hipEngine tracked memory remains correct and unchanged.
